one_line_ask: Build the RoadWise branded source-asset kit for the top-down traffic shorts: vectorize reusable road/vehicle/arrow icons to clean SVG, apply one consistent color grade across the reference frames, produce a 1080x1920 vertical cover/end-card and a stock-backed road texture, then clean the scratch VO and reframe the rough preview clip to vertical — all chained so the deliverable kit emerges from the supplied plates.
brief:
RoadWise™ is a modern driving-education brand (beginner, nervous, teen, and defensive drivers) producing mobile-first vertical shorts (1080x1920) of clean, top-down animated traffic explainers — lane changes, turns, parallel parking, highway merging, 4-way stops, hazard recognition. We are standing up a 10-template content system and need the reusable, brand-consistent SOURCE-ASSET PACKAGE that our After Effects animator will build the templates from. (We are NOT asking the connector to animate — keyframing stays with our AE artist. We need the upstream art kit + downstream delivery prep.)

Supplied by us: (1) a flat-rendered top-down reference frame of a 4-lane road junction (PNG, on a neutral asphalt background); (2) a separate "icon plate" PNG holding our reusable top-down car body, turn-arrow, and yield/stop-sign glyphs laid out on white; (3) our RoadWise wordmark/logo PNG (transparent); (4) a scratch voice-over WAV (a presenter explaining a 4-way-stop, recorded on a noisy laptop mic); (5) one rough ~12s After Effects preview render (16:9, 1920x1080) of a lane-change move that we want test-reframed to the vertical short canvas.

We need, as one chained job:
- A clean, scalable
